chunks.title: Introduction to Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The podcast begins with a discussion on the benefits of an animal-based diet, emphasizing the energy and health improvements experienced by the speaker. They introduce the guest, Ben Patrick, known for his work in the fitness community and as the 'Knees Over Toes Guy,' highlighting the positive impact of their collaboration.
chunks.transcript: but there's no doubt for me meat fruit then adding in Honey like I have all the energy I need to do what I want I feel great on this week's podcast I had my good friend Ben Patrick and even better than that Ben came to Costa Rica you may know Ben as knees over toes guy and his contributions to the exercise space are incredible he's helped me strengthen my knees I use so many of his movements and thinking about movements in my routine to keep my shoulders strong to keep my knees strong and so I'm so grateful that he came on the podcast for this conversation we talked about health we talked about where he's going in his life his next steps his next new projects which are Super exciting how he thinks about knees our sort of shared interest in thinking outside the box we also filmed a YouTube video about how to eat and how to train in a day we'll put a link in the description of this podcast when it's out when this podcast comes out that video will probably not be out but we'll link it when it is out so stay tuned for the YouTube collab between me and knees over toes guy Ben Patrick also animal based 30 has now

chunks.title: Living in Costa Rica
chunks.summary: The speakers share their experiences living in Costa Rica, discussing the natural abundance of food and the ease of living near the equator. They highlight the comfort of the climate, the availability of fresh food, and the benefits of a lifestyle that emphasizes connection to nature and community.

chunks.title: Basic Lifestyle Tenets for Health
chunks.summary: Discussing the fundamental aspects of a healthy lifestyle, they identify four essential tenets: diet, movement/exercise, connection to nature, and community. The conversation shifts to the importance of these elements in addressing mental health and overall wellbeing.
chunks.transcript: some nutrition movement exercise nature Community yeah I I agree I think those are four tenants and what I so appreciate about your work is that you've really taken the movement and exercise piece and thought about it in a way that very few have which is really cool so Props props for that yeah my main line of thinking over all these years has become how do we restore the Natural Balance the natural design of the body okay it's natural for performance that we try to go forward so to run faster to jump all these things and then it turns out that going backward moving the body backward becomes incredibly therapeutic for the knees and the feet and now training the tibialis muscle which gets neglected so over a lifetime of building all these muscles above now you have more weight down into the foot the shin the knee than nature intended we get out of these balances so like the the program we did we did a full exercise program for your YouTube channel all we're trying to do is actually restore natural balances yeah that's become my way of thinking about exercise the body was designed to work right so where have we deviated and then how can we use movement to balance out those flows I think about nutrition the same way the body is designed to be

chunks.summary: The dialogue deepens into dietary choices, emphasizing the importance of meat and fruit. The guests express their belief that a diet centered around these foods can lead to sustained energy and better health, alongside sharing personal anecdotes of dietary transformations.

chunks.title: Experiencing Dietary Benefits
chunks.summary: The speakers share personal experiences with dietary changes, particularly the shift from low-carb diets to including more fruit and animal products. They discuss the positive changes they noticed in energy levels and overall well-being, along with the challenges of adhering to various diets.

chunks.title: The Importance of Nutrient-Dense Foods
chunks.summary: The conversation emphasizes the role of nutrient-dense foods, particularly organ meats, in enhancing health and preventing chronic diseases. They discuss the nutritional value of liver and other organ meats, advocating for their inclusion in diets due to their vital nutrients.

chunks.title: Understanding Toxins in Food and Environment
chunks.summary: Exploring the impact of environmental toxins on health, the speakers stress the importance of food quality. They discuss the significance of consuming natural and minimally processed foods, warning against additives like seed oils and advocating for a clean diet.

chunks.title: The Need for Holistic Fitness Approaches
chunks.summary: They discuss the integration of strength and flexibility in fitness, emphasizing the need for a well-rounded approach to exercise. They stress the importance of addressing underlying imbalances in the body to achieve better health outcomes and avoid unnecessary surgeries.

chunks.title: Schooling and Nutrition Education
chunks.summary: As the podcast concludes, the speakers discuss the future of a new type of educational framework focused on practical life skills, nutrition, and health education. They highlight the importance of teaching children about proper dietary habits and fitness to foster healthier lifestyles.

chunks.title: Closing Remarks on Natural Living
chunks.summary: In the final segment, the conversation reflects on the importance of connecting with nature and embracing common sense approaches to health and nutrition. They close with a reiteration of the shared belief in the simplicity and vitality of natural living.
